source_reference.citation: Fawaz et al., Text-Based Personas for Simulating User Privacy Decisions, arXiv:2603.19791.
source_reference.extraction_notes: Targets are abstract-level topline metrics extracted from the frozen cycle_002 PDF. The abstract reports benchmarking across five datasets, up to 87% predictive accuracy, a 6-17 percentage point gain over a non-personalized LLM baseline, and an 80-95% reduction in prompt tokens versus in-context learning with raw examples.

population_definition.target_population: privacy-decision survey respondents represented across five privacy-centric benchmark datasets
population_definition.notes: This fixture captures paper-reported aggregate benchmark performance only, not representative human prevalence.

questionnaire_or_task.notes: Topline evaluation for text-based synthetic privacy personas grounded in prior privacy decisions.

actual_targets.label: Minimum reported gain over non-personalized LLM baseline
Maximum reported gain over non-personalized LLM baseline
